seoTitle: Memsensing Sensor Solutions | Automotive, Industrial, IoT Applications
seoDescription: Explore Memsensing sensor solutions for automotive TPMS, industrial process control, smart home, and IoT applications. Complete solutions with technical support.

faqs.question: What industries does Memsensing serve with its sensor solutions?
faqs.answer: Memsensing provides sensor solutions across multiple industries: 1) Automotive - TPMS, engine management, HVAC, and battery management systems with AEC-Q100 qualified sensors; 2) Industrial - process control, pneumatic/hydraulic monitoring, and condition-based maintenance with 4-20mA transmitters; 3) Smart Home - environmental monitoring, HVAC control, and appliance automation; 4) Consumer Electronics - smartphones, wearables, and IoT devices with compact, low-power sensors; 5) Medical - respiratory equipment and patient monitoring. Each industry solution leverages Memsensing's core MEMS technology with application-specific customization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your industry and application requirements, then explore our industry-specific solutions or contact our FAE team for customized recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I choose between raw sensors and complete transmitter modules?
faqs.answer: The choice between raw sensors and transmitter modules depends on your system design: 1) Raw sensors (MSP, MSM, MSA series) are ideal for OEMs with in-house electronics design capabilities, offering lower cost and design flexibility; 2) Transmitter modules (MSM-P series) provide complete solutions with signal conditioning, calibration, and standard industrial interfaces (4-20mA, 0-10V), perfect for quick integration and industrial applications; 3) Consider your team's expertise, development timeline, production volume, and interface requirements. Raw sensors suit high-volume consumer and automotive OEMs, while modules are preferred for industrial equipment and low-volume applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose raw sensors if you have design resources and need cost optimization. Select transmitter modules for faster time-to-market and standard industrial interfaces.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for Memsensing solution implementation?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Memsensing solution implementation: 1) Application engineering support for sensor selection and system integration; 2) Reference designs and evaluation kits for rapid prototyping; 3) Calibration and compensation algorithm consultation; 4) EMC testing guidance and troubleshooting; 5) Customization support for special requirements including pressure range, output type, and mechanical interface modifications; 6) Training programs for customer engineering teams; 7) Sample units for evaluation and testing. Our FAE team has deep expertise in MEMS technology across automotive, industrial, and consumer applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design phase for optimal sensor selection and integration guidance. Request evaluation kits for prototyping.

faqs.question: Can Memsensing sensors be customized for specific applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Memsensing offers extensive customization options: 1) Pressure range customization within sensor capabilities for unique measurement requirements; 2) Output signal configuration - analog voltage, 4-20mA current loop, or digital I2C/SPI/Modbus; 3) Mechanical interface customization including port type, thread specification, and package form factor; 4) Calibration and compensation algorithms for specific temperature ranges or accuracy requirements; 5) Special testing and screening procedures for high-reliability applications; 6) Module enclosure and connector customization. Customization typically requires minimum order quantities (MOQ 1000+ for sensors, 100+ for modules) and may involve NRE charges. Lead times range from 8-16 weeks depending on complexity.
faqs.decisionGuide: For customization requirements, contact our sales team with detailed specifications. Standard products suit most applications without customization.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Memsensing solutions compared to international brands?
faqs.answer: Memsensing solutions offer several competitive advantages: 1) Cost-effectiveness - typically 20-40% lower cost than equivalent international brands while maintaining comparable performance; 2) Local support - faster response times and direct access to engineering teams for Asia-Pacific customers; 3) Customization flexibility - shorter lead times for custom products and willingness to accommodate special requirements; 4) Supply chain stability - reduced geopolitical risks and shorter logistics lead times; 5) Technical performance - meets or exceeds industry standards with AEC-Q100 automotive qualification and industrial-grade reliability; 6) Growing ecosystem - expanding reference designs and application support. These advantages make Memsensing particularly attractive for cost-sensitive and high-volume applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate Memsensing solutions for your application through sample testing. Contact our FAE for side-by-side performance comparisons.

solutions.name: Automotive TPMS Solution
solutions.description: Complete tire pressure monitoring system solution featuring Memsensing MSP-100KPA-A pressure sensors with AEC-Q100 qualification, providing accurate tire pressure and temperature monitoring for enhanced vehicle safety and fuel efficiency.
features: AEC-Q100 qualified pressure sensors for automotive reliability
±1% full-scale accuracy across -40°C to +125°C temperature range
Low power consumption for extended battery life
Fast response time <1ms for real-time monitoring
Compact SOP-8 package suitable for valve stem mounting
Integrated temperature compensation for accurate readings

keyComponents.description: Automotive pressure sensor for tire pressure measurement
Digital output version with enhanced diagnostics
MEMS microphone for valve stem audio monitoring

coreAdvantages.title: Automotive-Grade Reliability
coreAdvantages.description: AEC-Q100 Grade 0 qualification ensures reliable operation in harsh automotive environments with temperature cycling, vibration, and mechanical shock resistance.

coreAdvantages.title: Superior Accuracy
coreAdvantages.description: ±1% full-scale accuracy across the entire automotive temperature range ensures precise tire pressure monitoring for safety and fuel efficiency optimization.

coreAdvantages.title: Ultra-Low Power Design
coreAdvantages.description: Sub-5uA sleep mode current consumption enables 5+ year battery life in valve-stem mounted TPMS units, reducing maintenance costs.

coreAdvantages.title: Fast Time-to-Market
coreAdvantages.description: Comprehensive technical documentation, reference designs, and FAE support accelerate TPMS system development and certification.

customerCases.challenge: The customer needed a cost-effective TPMS solution for their new passenger vehicle platform that met stringent automotive reliability requirements while reducing system cost compared to their existing international supplier. The solution needed to support both direct and indirect TPMS configurations.
customerCases.solution: LiTong provided the MSP-100KPA-A pressure sensor combined with comprehensive application support including reference designs, calibration guidelines, and EMC optimization recommendations. Our FAE team worked closely with the customer's engineering team to optimize the sensor integration and RF layout.
customerCases.results: The customer achieved 30% cost reduction compared to their previous solution while maintaining equivalent performance and reliability. The system passed all automotive qualification tests including AEC-Q100, EMC, and environmental testing. Production ramped to 500,000 units annually with <50ppm field failure rate.

customerCases.challenge: A fleet management company required a robust TPMS solution for heavy trucks operating in extreme conditions including temperature variations from -40°C to +85°C, high vibration, and exposure to road salt and chemicals. The system needed to integrate with their existing fleet management platform.
customerCases.solution: LiTong provided a customized TPMS solution based on the MSP-100KPA-A with enhanced environmental protection and extended temperature calibration. We also provided technical support for integration with their telematics system and developed custom firmware for their specific requirements.
customerCases.results: The TPMS solution has been deployed across 10,000+ vehicles with 99.7% uptime. The fleet reported 15% reduction in tire-related breakdowns and 8% improvement in fuel efficiency through optimized tire pressure management. The system has operated reliably for over 3 years in harsh conditions.

faeInsights.insight: Having supported dozens of TPMS designs over my career, I've learned that successful TPMS implementation requires attention to three critical areas: sensor accuracy, RF performance, and power management. The MSP-100KPA-A excels in all three areas with its ±1% accuracy, compact form factor that doesn't interfere with RF performance, and sub-5uA sleep current. I always recommend implementing proper EMI filtering on the power supply line and ensuring adequate thermal management in the valve-stem mounting. For RF design, keep the antenna away from metal components and follow the reference layout closely. The sensor's fast response time enables real-time monitoring without system lag. One common pitfall I see is insufficient temperature compensation - while the MSP-100KPA-A has excellent built-in compensation, proper PCB layout is essential to avoid thermal gradients that can affect accuracy. For customers transitioning from international brands, I recommend starting with a direct performance comparison using our evaluation kits - most are impressed by the equivalent performance at significantly lower cost.
faeInsights.logic: TPMS design follows a systematic approach: First, define your accuracy and temperature requirements based on application (passenger car vs commercial vehicle). Second, select the appropriate sensor output type - analog for simple systems, digital for smart TPMS with diagnostics. Third, design the RF subsystem with proper antenna placement and matching. Fourth, implement power management with aggressive sleep modes for battery life. Fifth, validate through environmental and EMC testing. The decision matrix considers cost, accuracy, battery life, and integration complexity. For high-volume passenger vehicles, the MSP-100KPA-A analog output offers the best balance. For premium vehicles requiring diagnostics, the digital version is preferred.
keyTakeaways: AEC-Q100 qualification is essential for automotive TPMS - never use consumer-grade sensors in safety applications
RF layout is as critical as sensor selection - follow reference designs closely for best performance
Power management determines battery life - implement aggressive sleep modes with periodic wake-up
Temperature compensation is built-in but proper PCB thermal design is still important
Always validate with environmental testing including temperature cycling and mechanical shock
commonPitfalls: Insufficient EMI protection on power supply lines leading to RF interference
Poor antenna placement causing reduced transmission range
Inadequate sealing leading to moisture ingress and corrosion
Overlooking self-test requirements in automotive safety standards
bestPractices: Implement two-stage power supply filtering with ferrite beads and capacitors
Use conformal coating on PCB for moisture protection in valve stem environment
Design antenna with adequate keep-out area from metal components
Include built-in self-test capability for safety system validation
Plan for calibration station integration in production line

faqs.question: What is the typical battery life for a TPMS unit using MSP-100KPA-A?
faqs.answer: TPMS battery life depends on transmission frequency, sleep current, and battery capacity. Typical configuration with MSP-100KPA-A: 1) Sleep current <5uA (sensor + microcontroller); 2) Transmission current 5-10mA for 10ms every 60 seconds; 3) CR2032 battery (225mAh capacity). Calculated battery life: Average current = 5uA + (10mA × 0.0167s/60s) = 7.8uA. Battery life = 225mAh / 7.8uA = 28,800 hours = 3.3 years. Optimizing to 5uA average with lower transmission frequency can achieve 5+ years. Actual life depends on duty cycle, temperature, and battery quality.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for <5uA sleep current and optimize transmission frequency for your application requirements. Use high-quality batteries for maximum life.

faqs.question: How do I ensure EMC compliance for TPMS modules?
faqs.answer: TPMS EMC compliance requires attention to both emissions and immunity: 1) Conducted emissions - implement proper power supply filtering with ferrite beads and capacitors; 2) Radiated emissions - follow RF layout best practices with proper antenna design and ground planes; 3) Radiated immunity - use shielding and filtering to protect sensitive analog circuits; 4) ESD protection - add TVS diodes on external connections; 5) Transient immunity - protect against load dump and other automotive transients. The MSP-100KPA-A has good EMC performance when properly integrated. Pre-compliance testing is recommended before formal certification testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow automotive EMC design guidelines and conduct pre-compliance testing early. Contact our FAE for EMC troubleshooting support.

faqs.question: Can the MSP-100KPA-A be used for indirect TPMS systems?
faqs.answer: While the MSP-100KPA-A is designed for direct TPMS (measuring actual tire pressure), it can also be used in indirect TPMS systems that estimate tire pressure through wheel speed analysis. However, indirect TPMS typically doesn't require pressure sensors in the wheel - it uses existing ABS wheel speed sensors. For hybrid systems that combine direct and indirect monitoring, the MSP-100KPA-A provides the direct pressure measurement component. The sensor's fast response and accuracy make it suitable for both direct TPMS and as a reference for indirect system calibration.
faqs.decisionGuide: For indirect TPMS, you typically don't need pressure sensors. Use MSP-100KPA-A for direct TPMS or hybrid systems requiring actual pressure measurement.

faqs.question: What calibration is required for TPMS production?
faqs.answer: TPMS production calibration typically includes: 1) Pressure calibration - apply known pressures (0, 50, 100kPa) and record sensor output; 2) Temperature calibration - test at multiple temperature points if required; 3) RF calibration - verify transmission power and frequency; 4) ID programming - assign unique sensor ID. The MSP-100KPA-A is factory-calibrated, but system-level calibration may be needed for highest accuracy. Most customers implement a two-point pressure calibration (zero and span) at production. Calibration equipment includes pressure standards, temperature chambers, and RF test equipment. LiTong provides calibration guidelines and can recommend equipment suppliers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement at least two-point pressure calibration in production. Contact our FAE for detailed calibration procedures and equipment recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I handle sensor replacement and pairing in TPMS?
faqs.answer: TPMS sensor replacement and pairing procedures: 1) Each sensor has a unique ID programmed during production; 2) Vehicle ECU learns sensor IDs through a learn/pairing procedure; 3) Common methods include: OBD-II tool activation, magnet activation, or automatic learning during drive; 4) When replacing sensors, the new sensor IDs must be learned by the ECU; 5) Some systems require all four sensors to be replaced/learned simultaneously. LiTong provides sensor IDs in barcode format for easy scanning during installation. Our FAE team can provide guidance on implementing learn procedures for specific vehicle platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement a sensor learning procedure compatible with your vehicle platform. Contact our FAE for ID format and learning procedure recommendations.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between valve-stem and strap-mounted TPMS sensors?
faqs.answer: Valve-stem and strap-mounted TPMS sensors have different design considerations: 1) Valve-stem mounting - sensor attached to valve stem, compact design, requires valve replacement, direct air pressure access; 2) Strap/band mounting - sensor strapped to wheel rim, larger package, no valve modification, may require air tube connection. The MSP-100KPA-A in SOP-8 package is suitable for valve-stem mounting due to its compact size. Strap-mounted designs often use larger packages with more features. Valve-stem mounting is more common in passenger vehicles due to compact size and easy installation. Strap mounting is used in some commercial vehicles and retrofit applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose valve-stem mounting for compact OEM designs. Consider strap mounting for retrofit or special applications. The MSP-100KPA-A suits valve-stem designs.

solutions.name: Industrial Process Control Solution
solutions.description: Comprehensive pressure monitoring solution for industrial process control applications featuring Memsensing transmitter modules with 4-20mA output, IP67 enclosures, and robust stainless steel construction for reliable operation in harsh industrial environments.
features: Complete transmitter with integrated signal conditioning
IP67 enclosure for harsh industrial environments
316L stainless steel construction for media compatibility

coreAdvantages.title: Complete Integrated Solution
coreAdvantages.description: Integrated sensor, signal conditioning, and transmitter electronics eliminate external components, reducing system complexity and installation time.

coreAdvantages.title: Industrial Standard Interface
coreAdvantages.description: 4-20mA current loop output is compatible with virtually all industrial PLCs, controllers, and SCADA systems, enabling plug-and-play integration.

coreAdvantages.title: Harsh Environment Reliability
coreAdvantages.description: IP67 enclosure and 316L stainless steel construction ensure reliable operation in wet, dusty, and corrosive industrial environments.

coreAdvantages.title: Cost-Effective Alternative
coreAdvantages.description: Offers performance comparable to established industrial brands at 30-40% lower cost, reducing system cost without compromising reliability.

customerCases.challenge: A hydraulic equipment manufacturer needed reliable pressure monitoring for their line of industrial hydraulic presses operating at pressures up to 350 bar. Their existing pressure transmitters were experiencing failures due to pressure spikes and vibration, leading to costly downtime and maintenance.
customerCases.solution: LiTong provided the MSM-P1000-420 high-pressure transmitter with its robust 316L stainless steel construction and overpressure protection. Our FAE team assisted with proper installation procedures including pressure snubbers to protect against water hammer effects.
customerCases.results: The customer reduced pressure transmitter failures by 85% and improved system uptime significantly. The 4-20mA output integrated seamlessly with their existing PLC control system. Over 500 units have been deployed across their press product line with excellent reliability over 2+ years of operation.

customerCases.challenge: A municipal water treatment plant needed pressure monitoring for their filtration system to measure differential pressure across filter membranes. The environment was wet and corrosive with chlorine exposure, requiring IP67 protection and chemical-resistant materials.
customerCases.solution: LiTong supplied the MSM-P400-420 transmitter with IP67 enclosure and 316L stainless steel wetted parts. We provided guidance on differential pressure measurement setup using two transmitters and calculated the pressure drop across filters.
customerCases.results: The pressure monitoring system has operated reliably for over 18 months in the harsh water treatment environment. The plant operators can now monitor filter condition in real-time and schedule maintenance based on actual pressure drop rather than fixed schedules, reducing maintenance costs by 25% and improving water quality consistency.

faeInsights.insight: In my 15 years supporting industrial sensor applications, I've learned that successful pressure transmitter deployment requires attention to three key areas: proper sizing, installation practices, and environmental protection. For sizing, always select a pressure range that provides at least 1.5x safety margin above your maximum operating pressure - this protects against pressure spikes and transients common in hydraulic systems. The MSM-P400-420 and MSM-P1000-420 have excellent overpressure ratings, but proper sizing still matters for best accuracy. For installation, use appropriate thread sealant rated for your media and torque to specifications - I've seen many leaks from improper sealing. For harsh environments, the IP67 rating of these transmitters provides excellent protection, but proper cable gland installation is essential. One common mistake I see is using standard cable glands instead of IP67-rated ones, compromising the enclosure protection. Another issue is mounting orientation - while the MEMS sensor isn't sensitive to orientation, the electrical connection should be protected from direct spray or immersion. For troubleshooting, remember that 4-20mA loops are generally very reliable - most issues are wiring-related. Always verify power supply voltage at the transmitter terminals, not just at the power supply output.
faeInsights.logic: Industrial pressure monitoring design follows these steps: First, define your pressure range with appropriate safety margin and select the transmitter model (MSM-P400-420 for 0-400kPa, MSM-P1000-420 for 0-1MPa). Second, verify media compatibility with 316L stainless steel wetted parts. Third, determine process connection type (G1/4 standard, adapters available for NPT). Fourth, calculate loop resistance to ensure it's within transmitter drive capability. Fifth, plan cable routing with proper shielding and protection. Sixth, install with proper thread sealant and torque. The decision matrix considers pressure range, media compatibility, environmental protection, and output signal requirements. For most industrial applications, the 4-20mA output provides the best balance of noise immunity and compatibility.
keyTakeaways: Always size pressure transmitters with 1.5x safety margin above maximum operating pressure
Use proper thread sealant rated for your media and torque to specifications
Verify loop resistance is within transmitter drive capability (typically <500 ohm at 24V)
Install IP67 cable glands properly to maintain environmental protection
For hydraulic applications with pressure spikes, consider adding pressure snubbers
commonPitfalls: Insufficient pressure range margin leading to overpressure damage
Using wrong thread sealant causing leaks or media contamination
Excessive loop resistance causing transmitter output to saturate
Improper cable gland installation compromising IP67 protection
Mounting near heat sources causing temperature drift
bestPractices: Install isolation valves for transmitter removal without system shutdown
Use pressure snubbers in applications with rapid valve operation
Implement regular calibration verification as part of maintenance schedule
Document installation details including orientation and process conditions
Plan for spare transmitters in critical applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum cable length for 4-20mA transmitters?
faqs.answer: Maximum cable length depends on supply voltage and total loop resistance: 1) At 24V supply, transmitter can drive up to 500 ohm load; 2) Typical 18 AWG cable has 6.5 ohms/1000ft resistance; 3) PLC input resistor is typically 250 ohms; 4) Remaining budget for cable: 500 - 250 = 250 ohms; 5) Maximum cable length: 250 / 6.5 × 1000 = 38,000 ft (theoretical). Practical limits are typically 1000-2000 feet due to noise pickup and capacitance. Use shielded twisted pair cable for noise immunity. For very long runs, increase supply voltage or use signal repeaters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total loop resistance including cable, load resistor, and any additional devices. Verify it's within transmitter drive capability.

faqs.question: How do I troubleshoot a 4-20mA transmitter reading incorrectly?
faqs.answer: Troubleshooting steps for incorrect 4-20mA readings: 1) Verify power supply voltage at transmitter terminals (12-30VDC); 2) Check loop continuity with multimeter; 3) Verify transmitter is properly grounded; 4) Check for ground loops - there should be only one ground point in the loop; 5) Verify process pressure is within transmitter range; 6) Test transmitter by venting to atmosphere - should read 4mA; 7) Check for electrical noise with oscilloscope; 8) Verify PLC scaling is correct. Common issues include: power supply voltage drop under load, ground loops causing offset, incorrect PLC scaling, and process connection blockage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow systematic troubleshooting starting with power supply and loop continuity. Contact our technical support if issues persist.

faqs.question: Can the transmitter be used with food-grade applications?
faqs.answer: The standard MSM-P400-420 and MSM-P1000-420 use 316L stainless steel wetted parts which are generally suitable for food applications. However, for direct food contact applications, additional requirements may include: 1) FDA compliance documentation; 2) 3A sanitary certification for dairy applications; 3) Surface finish requirements (Ra < 0.8 μm typically); 4) Sanitary process connections (Tri-Clamp, DIN 11851); 5) Food-grade fill fluid if applicable. Memsensing can provide FDA compliance letters and may offer sanitary versions with appropriate connections. Contact our sales team for food-grade application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard 316L stainless steel is suitable for many food applications. Contact our sales team for FDA documentation or sanitary connection requirements.

faqs.question: What maintenance is required for industrial pressure transmitters?
faqs.answer: Industrial pressure transmitters require minimal maintenance: 1) Periodic visual inspection of enclosure and cable connections; 2) Check process connection for leaks; 3) Verify output signal at known pressure points annually (optional verification); 4) Clean external surfaces as needed; 5) No internal maintenance required. The solid-state MEMS sensor has no moving parts and excellent long-term stability. Recommended maintenance schedule: Visual inspection monthly, calibration verification annually (if required by quality system). The transmitter's stability specification is ±0.2% FS/year, so drift is minimal. Expected service life is 10+ years in normal industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimal maintenance required. Perform visual inspections per your facility maintenance schedule. Calibration verification optional unless required by quality system.

faqs.question: How do I protect the transmitter from pressure spikes?
faqs.answer: Pressure spike protection methods: 1) Select transmitter with appropriate overpressure rating (MSM series has 1.5x FS safe overpressure, 3x FS burst); 2) Install pressure snubber/dampener in pressure line; 3) Use pulsation dampener for reciprocating pump applications; 4) Install pressure relief valve to limit maximum system pressure; 5) Position transmitter away from rapid valve operation if possible. Pressure snubbers are recommended for hydraulic applications with fast-acting valves. The snubber acts as a restrictor, damping pressure transients while allowing normal pressure measurement. Select snubber based on your application's response time requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Install pressure snubbers in applications with rapid valve operation or pressure pulsations. Select appropriate overpressure rating for your application.

faqs.question: Can multiple transmitters be powered from the same power supply?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple 4-20mA transmitters can share a common power supply provided it has adequate capacity: 1) Calculate total current requirement: Each transmitter draws up to 20mA (actually ~25mA at 24V including overhead); 2) Add margin for startup and safety (typically 20%); 3) Example: 10 transmitters × 25mA = 250mA; With 20% margin = 300mA minimum supply capacity; 4) Use a 24VDC, 1A supply for this example. Important considerations: 1) Each transmitter needs its own current loop to the PLC; 2) Common power supply is fine, but loops must be independent; 3) Use proper wire sizing for total current; 4) Consider voltage drop in power distribution wiring.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total current requirement with margin and size power supply accordingly. Each transmitter needs independent current loop to PLC.
